sections.heading: Create the MongoDB Search Index
Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
sections.body:
autocomplete

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that begin with `haw`.

```json
{
  "autocomplete": {
    "query": "haw",
    "path": "plot"
  }
}
```

phrase

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains the phrase `new york` with a maximum distance of 5 between terms.

```json
{
  "phrase": {
    "query": "new york",
    "path": "plot",
    "slop": 5
  }
}
```

regex

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the regular expression pattern.

```json
{
  "regex": {
    "query": "(?i)new.*york",
    "path": "plot"
  }
}
```

wildcard

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the wildcard pattern.

```json
{
  "wildcard": {
    "query": "new*york",
    "path": "plot"
  }
}
```

MongoDB Search returns the search results for documents in the `movies` collection.

This tutorial describes how to create an index on the
`sample_mflix.movies` collection and run partial string queries
against the `plot` field using MongoDB Compass.

To return matches for partial string queries, you can use one of the following operators:

- The [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for a word or phrase that contains the sequence of
  characters that you specify with your query.

- The [Phrase](phrase-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for documents that contain the terms in your query
  string at the distance you specify between the terms.

- The [regex](regex-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for strings using regular expression.

- The [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields using special characters in your query to match any
  character.

You can also use the [text](text-ref) operator with a custom analyzer
for more fine-grained control over partial matching. To learn more, see
[Custom analyzers](custom-analyzers).

This tutorial takes you through the following steps:

- Set up a MongoDB Search index on the `plot` field in the
  `sample_mflix.movies` collection.

- Run MongoDB Search query for a partial string against the `plot` field in
  the `sample_mflix.movies` collection using
  [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref), [Phrase](phrase-ref), [regex](regex-ref), and
  [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operators.

Before you begin, ensure that your cluster meets the following requirements.

To complete the tutorials, you must have:

- An Atlas cluster with MongoDB version 6.0 or higher or a MongoDB
  self-managed cluster with MongoDB version 8.2 or higher.

- The [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data) loaded into your
  cluster.

- [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) or higher access to your project to create MongoDB Search indexes.

- [Atlas UI](fts-tutorial-run-query), [mongosh](mongodb-shell), [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass), or a supported
  [MongoDB Driver](drivers) to run queries on your cluster.

> **Note:** You can run MongoDB Vector Search queries by using any driver
> through the $vectorSearch aggregation stage.
> These tutorials include examples for a selection
> of clients. Refer to the specific tutorial page for details.
>
> You can also complete these tutorials with local deployments that you create
> with the Atlas CLI or with an on-prem deployment. To learn more, see
> [Create a Local Atlas Deployment](atlas/cli/current/atlas-cli-deploy-local) and
> [Self-Managed Deployments](manual/self-managed-deployments).

To learn more about how to implement partial matches, see [MongoDB Text Search: Substring Pattern Matching Including Regex and Wildcard, Use Search Instead](https://medium.com/mongodb/mongodb-text-search-substring-pattern-matching-including-regex-and-wildcard-use-search-instead-3633c6f7e604).

sections.heading: Create the MongoDB Search Index
Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
sections.body:
Open [mongosh](mongodb-shell) in a terminal window and connect to your
cluster. For detailed instructions on connecting, see
[MongoDB Shell](connect-mongo-shell).

Run the following command at the [mongosh](mongodb-shell) prompt:

```javascript
use sample_mflix
```

autocomplete

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that begin with `haw`.

```javascript
db.movies.aggregate([
  {
    $search: {
      "index": "partial-match-tutorial-autocomplete",
      "autocomplete": {
        "path": "plot",
        "query": "off"
      }
    }
  }
])

```

phrase

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains the phrase `new york` with a maximum distance of 5 between terms.

```javascript
db.movies.aggregate([
  {
    $search: {
      "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
      "phrase": {
        "path": "plot",
        "query": "off"
      }
    }
  }
])

```

regex

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the regular expression pattern.

```javascript
db.movies.aggregate([
  {
    $search: {
      "regex": "partial-match-tutorial",
      "phrase": {
        "path": "plot",
        "query": "off"
      }
    }
  }
])

```

wildcard

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the wildcard pattern.

```javascript
db.movies.aggregate([
  {
    $search: {
      "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
      "wildcard": {
        "path": "plot",
        "query": "how*"
      }
    }
  }
])

```

This tutorial describes how to create an index on the
`sample_mflix.movies` collection and run partial string queries
against the `plot` field using the C driver.

To return matches for partial string queries, you can use one of the following operators:

- The [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for a word or phrase that contains the sequence of
  characters that you specify with your query.

- The [Phrase](phrase-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for documents that contain the terms in your query
  string at the distance you specify between the terms.

- The [regex](regex-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for strings using regular expression.

- The [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields using special characters in your query to match any
  character.

You can also use the [text](text-ref) operator with a custom analyzer
for more fine-grained control over partial matching. To learn more, see
[Custom analyzers](custom-analyzers).

This tutorial takes you through the following steps:

- Set up a MongoDB Search index on the `plot` field in the
  `sample_mflix.movies` collection.

- Run MongoDB Search query for a partial string against the `plot` field in
  the `sample_mflix.movies` collection using
  [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref), [Phrase](phrase-ref), [regex](regex-ref), and
  [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operators.

Before you begin, ensure that your cluster meets the following requirements.

To complete the tutorials, you must have:

- An Atlas cluster with MongoDB version 6.0 or higher or a MongoDB
  self-managed cluster with MongoDB version 8.2 or higher.

- The [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data) loaded into your
  cluster.

- [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) or higher access to your project to create MongoDB Search indexes.

- [Atlas UI](fts-tutorial-run-query), [mongosh](mongodb-shell), [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass), or a supported
  [MongoDB Driver](drivers) to run queries on your cluster.

> **Note:** You can run MongoDB Vector Search queries by using any driver
> through the $vectorSearch aggregation stage.
> These tutorials include examples for a selection
> of clients. Refer to the specific tutorial page for details.
>
> You can also complete these tutorials with local deployments that you create
> with the Atlas CLI or with an on-prem deployment. To learn more, see
> [Create a Local Atlas Deployment](atlas/cli/current/atlas-cli-deploy-local) and
> [Self-Managed Deployments](manual/self-managed-deployments).

To learn more about how to implement partial matches, see [MongoDB Text Search: Substring Pattern Matching Including Regex and Wildcard, Use Search Instead](https://medium.com/mongodb/mongodb-text-search-substring-pattern-matching-including-regex-and-wildcard-use-search-instead-3633c6f7e604).

sections.heading: Create the MongoDB Search Index
Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
Create the MongoDB Search Index
Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
Create the MongoDB Search Index

codeBlocks.content:
# Create a new directory and initialize the project
mkdir atlas-search-partial-match && cd atlas-search-partial-match
go mod init atlas-search-partial-match

# Add the MongoDB Go Driver to your project
go get go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/mongo

go run create_auto_complete_index.go
# or
go run create_string_index.go

sections.heading: Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
Create the MongoDB Search Index
sections.body:
- Install the MongoDB Python Driver.

  ```shell
  pip install pymongo
  ```

  For detailed installation instructions, see
  [MongoDB Python Driver documentation](languages/python/pymongo-driver/get-started).

- Create the index.

  autocomplete

  Create a file named `create_auto_complete_index.py` and paste the following code:

  ```python
  from pymongo import MongoClient

  # connect to your Atlas deployment
  uri = "<connection-string>"

  client = MongoClient(uri)

  def run():
      try:
          # set namespace
          database = client["sample_mflix"]
          collection = database["movies"]

      # define your MongoDB Search index
          index = {
              "name": "partial-match-tutorial-autocomplete",
              "definition": {
                  # search index definition fields
                  "mappings": {
                      "dynamic": False,
                      "fields": {
                          "title": {
                              "type": "autocomplete",
                              "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
                              "tokenization": "edgeGram",
                              "minGrams": 3,
                              "maxGrams": 5,
                              "foldDiacritics": False
                          }
                      }
                  }
              }
          }

          # run the helper method
          result = collection.create_search_index(index)
          print(f"New index name: {result}")
      finally:
          client.close()

  if __name__ == "__main__":
      run()

  ```

  string

  Create a file named `create_string_index.py` and paste the following code:

  ```python
  from pymongo import MongoClient

  # connect to your Atlas deployment
  uri = "<connection-string>"

  client = MongoClient(uri)

  def run():
      try:
          # set namespace
          database = client["sample_mflix"]
          collection = database["movies"]

      # define your MongoDB Search index
          index = {
              "name": "partial-match-tutorial",
              "definition": {
                  # search index definition fields
                  "mappings": {
                      "dynamic": False,
                      "fields": {
                          "title": {
                              "type": "string",
                          }
                      }
                  }
              }
          }

          # run the helper method
          result = collection.create_search_index(index)
          print(f"New index name: {result}")
      finally:
          client.close()

  if __name__ == "__main__":
      run()

  ```

  Atlas Cluster

  Replace `<connection-string>` with the connection string for your
  Atlas cluster or local Atlas deployment.

  Atlas Cluster

  Your connection string should use the following format:

  ```
  mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@<clusterName>.<hostname>.mongodb.net
  ```

  To learn more, see [Driver](connect-via-driver).

  Local or Self-Managed

  Your connection string should use the
  following format:

  ```
  mongodb://localhost:<port-number>/?directConnection=true
  ```

  To learn more, see [Connection Strings](manual/reference/connection-string).

  IMPORTANT: Ensure that your connection string includes your database
  user's credentials. To learn more about finding your connection string,
  see [Driver](connect-via-driver).

  Local Deployment

  Your connection string should use the following format:

  ```
  mongodb://localhost:<port-number>/?directConnection=true
  ```

- Run the script.

  ```shell
  python create_auto_complete_index.py
  # or
  python create_string_index.py
  ```

sections.heading: Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
sections.body:
autocomplete

The following query searches for documents where the `title` field
contains words that begin with `Great`.

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient

# connect to your Atlas cluster
uri = "<connection-string>"

client = MongoClient(uri)

def run():
    try:
        # set namespace
        database = client["sample_mflix"]
        coll = database["movies"]

        # define pipeline
        agg = [
            {
                "$search": {
                    "index": "partial-match-tutorial-autocomplete",
                    "autocomplete": {
                        "query": "Great",
                        "path": "title"
                    }
                }
            },
            {"$limit": 10},
            {"$project": {"_id": 0, "title": 1}}
        ]

        # run pipeline
        result = coll.aggregate(agg)
        print(result)

        # print results
        for doc in result:
            print(doc)
    finally:
        client.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

```

phrase

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains the phrase `new york` with a maximum distance of 5 between terms.

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient

# connect to your Atlas cluster
uri = "<connection-string>"

client = MongoClient(uri)

def run():
    try:
        # set namespace
        database = client["sample_mflix"]
        coll = database["movies"]

        # define pipeline
        agg = [
            {
                "$search": {
                    "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
                    "phrase": {
                        "path": "plot",
                        "query": "new york",
                    }
                }
            },
            {
                "$project": {
                    "_id": 0,
                    "title": 1
                }
            }
        ]

        # run pipeline
        result = coll.aggregate(agg)
        print(result)

        # print results
        for doc in result:
            print(doc)
    finally:
        client.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

```

regex

The following query searches for documents where the `title` field
contains words that match the regular expression pattern.

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient

# connect to your Atlas cluster
uri = "<connection-string>"

client = MongoClient(uri)

def run():
    try:
        # set namespace
        database = client["sample_mflix"]
        coll = database["movies"]

        # define pipeline
        agg = [
            {
                "$search": {
                    "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
                    "regex": {
                        "path": "title",
                        "query": "[0-9]{2} (.){4}s",
                        "allowAnalyzedField": True,
                    }
                }
            },
            {
                "$project": {
                    "_id": 0,
                    "title": 1
                }
            }
        ]

        # run pipeline
        result = coll.aggregate(agg)
        print(result)

        # print results
        for doc in result:
            print(doc)
    finally:
        client.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

```

wildcard

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the wildcard pattern.

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient

# connect to your Atlas cluster
uri = "<connection-string>"

client = MongoClient(uri)

def run():
    try:
        # set namespace
        database = client["sample_mflix"]
        coll = database["movies"]

        # define pipeline
        agg = [
            {
                "$search": {
                    "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
                    "wildcard": {
                        "path": "title",
                        "query": "how*",
                        "allowAnalyzedField": True,
                    }
                }
            },
            {
                "$project": {
                    "_id": 0,
                    "title": 1
                }
            }
        ]

        # run pipeline
        result = coll.aggregate(agg)
        print(result)

        # print results
        for doc in result:
            print(doc)
    finally:
        client.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

```

Ensure that your connection string includes your database user's
credentials. To learn more, see [Driver](connect-via-driver).

```bash
python partial-match-tutorial-query.py
```

This tutorial describes how to create an index on the
`sample_mflix.movies` collection and run partial string queries
against the `plot` field using the C# driver.

To return matches for partial string queries, you can use one of the following operators:

- The [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for a word or phrase that contains the sequence of
  characters that you specify with your query.

- The [Phrase](phrase-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for documents that contain the terms in your query
  string at the distance you specify between the terms.

- The [regex](regex-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for strings using regular expression.

- The [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields using special characters in your query to match any
  character.

You can also use the [text](text-ref) operator with a custom analyzer
for more fine-grained control over partial matching. To learn more, see
[Custom analyzers](custom-analyzers).

This tutorial takes you through the following steps:

- Set up a MongoDB Search index on the `plot` field in the
  `sample_mflix.movies` collection.

- Run MongoDB Search query for a partial string against the `plot` field in
  the `sample_mflix.movies` collection using
  [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref), [Phrase](phrase-ref), [regex](regex-ref), and
  [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operators.

Before you begin, ensure that your cluster meets the following requirements.

To complete the tutorials, you must have:

- An Atlas cluster with MongoDB version 6.0 or higher or a MongoDB
  self-managed cluster with MongoDB version 8.2 or higher.

- The [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data) loaded into your
  cluster.

- [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) or higher access to your project to create MongoDB Search indexes.

- [Atlas UI](fts-tutorial-run-query), [mongosh](mongodb-shell), [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass), or a supported
  [MongoDB Driver](drivers) to run queries on your cluster.

> **Note:** You can run MongoDB Vector Search queries by using any driver
> through the $vectorSearch aggregation stage.
> These tutorials include examples for a selection
> of clients. Refer to the specific tutorial page for details.
>
> You can also complete these tutorials with local deployments that you create
> with the Atlas CLI or with an on-prem deployment. To learn more, see
> [Create a Local Atlas Deployment](atlas/cli/current/atlas-cli-deploy-local) and
> [Self-Managed Deployments](manual/self-managed-deployments).

To learn more about how to implement partial matches, see [MongoDB Text Search: Substring Pattern Matching Including Regex and Wildcard, Use Search Instead](https://medium.com/mongodb/mongodb-text-search-substring-pattern-matching-including-regex-and-wildcard-use-search-instead-3633c6f7e604).

- Install the MongoDB Python Driver.

  ```shell
  pip install pymongo
  ```

  For detailed installation instructions, see

- Create the index.

  autocomplete

  Create a file named `create_auto_complete_index.py` and paste the following code:

  ```python
  from pymongo import MongoClient

  # connect to your Atlas deployment

  client = MongoClient(uri)

  def run():
      try:
          # set namespace

      # define your MongoDB Search index
          index = {
              "name": "partial-match-tutorial-autocomplete",
              "definition": {
                  # search index definition fields

                      "dynamic": False,
                      "fields": {
                          "title": {
  ```

  ```python
      # define your MongoDB Search index
          index = {
              "name": "partial-match-tutorial",
              "definition": {
                  # search index definition fields
                  "mappings": {
                      "dynamic": False,

                          "title": {
  ```

  following format:

  IMPORTANT: Ensure that your connection string includes your database

  see [Driver](connect-via-driver).

  Local Deployment

  Your connection string should use the following format:

  ```
  mongodb://localhost:<port-number>/?directConnection=true
  ```

- Run the script.

  ```shell
  python create_auto_complete_index.py
  # or
  python create_string_index.py
  ```

sections.heading: Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
sections.body:
autocomplete

The following query searches for documents where the `title` field
contains words that begin with `Great`.

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient

# connect to your Atlas cluster
uri = "<connection-string>"

client = MongoClient(uri)

def run():
    try:
        # set namespace
        database = client["sample_mflix"]
        coll = database["movies"]

        # define pipeline
        agg = [
            {
                "$search": {
                    "index": "partial-match-tutorial-autocomplete",
                    "autocomplete": {
                        "query": "Great",
                        "path": "title"
                    }
                }
            },
            {"$limit": 10},
            {"$project": {"_id": 0, "title": 1}}
        ]

        # run pipeline
        result = coll.aggregate(agg)
        print(result)

        # print results
        for doc in result:
            print(doc)
    finally:
        client.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

```

phrase

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains the phrase `new york` with a maximum distance of 5 between terms.

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient

# connect to your Atlas cluster
uri = "<connection-string>"

client = MongoClient(uri)

def run():
    try:
        # set namespace
        database = client["sample_mflix"]
        coll = database["movies"]

        # define pipeline
        agg = [
            {
                "$search": {
                    "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
                    "phrase": {
                        "path": "plot",
                        "query": "new york",
                    }
                }
            },
            {
                "$project": {
                    "_id": 0,
                    "title": 1
                }
            }
        ]

        # run pipeline
        result = coll.aggregate(agg)
        print(result)

        # print results
        for doc in result:
            print(doc)
    finally:
        client.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

```

regex

The following query searches for documents where the `title` field
contains words that match the regular expression pattern.

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient

# connect to your Atlas cluster
uri = "<connection-string>"

client = MongoClient(uri)

def run():
    try:
        # set namespace
        database = client["sample_mflix"]
        coll = database["movies"]

        # define pipeline
        agg = [
            {
                "$search": {
                    "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
                    "regex": {
                        "path": "title",
                        "query": "[0-9]{2} (.){4}s",
                        "allowAnalyzedField": True,
                    }
                }
            },
            {
                "$project": {
                    "_id": 0,
                    "title": 1
                }
            }
        ]

        # run pipeline
        result = coll.aggregate(agg)
        print(result)

        # print results
        for doc in result:
            print(doc)
    finally:
        client.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

```

wildcard

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the wildcard pattern.

```python
from pymongo import MongoClient

# connect to your Atlas cluster
uri = "<connection-string>"

client = MongoClient(uri)

def run():
    try:
        # set namespace
        database = client["sample_mflix"]
        coll = database["movies"]

        # define pipeline
        agg = [
            {
                "$search": {
                    "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
                    "wildcard": {
                        "path": "title",
                        "query": "how*",
                        "allowAnalyzedField": True,
                    }
                }
            },
            {
                "$project": {
                    "_id": 0,
                    "title": 1
                }
            }
        ]

        # run pipeline
        result = coll.aggregate(agg)
        print(result)

        # print results
        for doc in result:
            print(doc)
    finally:
        client.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

```

Ensure that your connection string includes your database user's
credentials. To learn more, see [Driver](connect-via-driver).

```bash
python partial-match-tutorial-query.py
```

This tutorial describes how to create an index on the
`sample_mflix.movies` collection and run partial string queries
against the `plot` field using the Atlas CLI in a local deployment.

To return matches for partial string queries, you can use one of the following operators:

- The [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for a word or phrase that contains the sequence of
  characters that you specify with your query.

- The [Phrase](phrase-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for documents that contain the terms in your query
  string at the distance you specify between the terms.

- The [regex](regex-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for strings using regular expression.

- The [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields using special characters in your query to match any
  character.

You can also use the [text](text-ref) operator with a custom analyzer
for more fine-grained control over partial matching. To learn more, see
[Custom analyzers](custom-analyzers).

This tutorial takes you through the following steps:

- Set up a MongoDB Search index on the `plot` field in the
  `sample_mflix.movies` collection.

- Run MongoDB Search query for a partial string against the `plot` field in
  the `sample_mflix.movies` collection using
  [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref), [Phrase](phrase-ref), [regex](regex-ref), and
  [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operators.

Before you begin, ensure that your cluster meets the following requirements.

To complete the tutorials, you must have:

- An Atlas cluster with MongoDB version 6.0 or higher or a MongoDB
  self-managed cluster with MongoDB version 8.2 or higher.

- The [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data) loaded into your
  cluster.

- [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) or higher access to your project to create MongoDB Search indexes.

- [Atlas UI](fts-tutorial-run-query), [mongosh](mongodb-shell), [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass), or a supported
  [MongoDB Driver](drivers) to run queries on your cluster.

> **Note:** You can run MongoDB Vector Search queries by using any driver
> through the $vectorSearch aggregation stage.
> These tutorials include examples for a selection
> of clients. Refer to the specific tutorial page for details.
>
> You can also complete these tutorials with local deployments that you create
> with the Atlas CLI or with an on-prem deployment. To learn more, see
> [Create a Local Atlas Deployment](atlas/cli/current/atlas-cli-deploy-local) and
> [Self-Managed Deployments](manual/self-managed-deployments).

To learn more about how to implement partial matches, see [MongoDB Text Search: Substring Pattern Matching Including Regex and Wildcard, Use Search Instead](https://medium.com/mongodb/mongodb-text-search-substring-pattern-matching-including-regex-and-wildcard-use-search-instead-3633c6f7e604).

sections.heading: Create the MongoDB Search Index
sections.body:
Run the following Atlas CLI command to create the search index:

```bash
atlas clusters search indexes create \
  --clusterName <clusterName> \
  --db sample_mflix \
  --collection movies \
  --field mappings.fields.plot.type=string \
  --indexName partial-match-tutorial
```

> **Note:** Before running this command, ensure that you have:
>
> - [Installed and configured the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current)
>
> - Authenticated with your Atlas account using `atlas auth login`
>
> - Set your project context using `atlas config set project_id <projectId>`

codeBlocks.content:
atlas clusters search indexes create \
  --clusterName <clusterName> \
  --db sample_mflix \
  --collection movies \
  --field mappings.fields.plot.type=string \
  --indexName partial-match-tutorial

sections.heading: Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
sections.body:
autocomplete

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that begin with `haw`.

```json
{
  "autocomplete": {
    "query": "haw",
    "path": "plot"
  }
}
```

phrase

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains the phrase `new york` with a maximum distance of 5 between terms.

```json
{
  "phrase": {
    "query": "new york",
    "path": "plot",
    "slop": 5
  }
}
```

regex

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the regular expression pattern.

```json
{
  "regex": {
    "query": "(?i)new.*york",
    "path": "plot"
  }
}
```

wildcard

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the wildcard pattern.

```json
{
  "wildcard": {
    "query": "new*york",
    "path": "plot"
  }
}
```

sections.body:
Open [mongosh](mongodb-shell) in a terminal window and connect to your
cluster. For detailed instructions on connecting, see
[MongoDB Shell](connect-mongo-shell).

Run the following command at the [mongosh](mongodb-shell) prompt:

```javascript
use sample_mflix
```

autocomplete

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that begin with `haw`.

```javascript
db.movies.aggregate([
  {
    $search: {
      "index": "partial-match-tutorial-autocomplete",
      "autocomplete": {
        "path": "plot",
        "query": "off"
      }
    }
  }
])

```

phrase

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains the phrase `new york` with a maximum distance of 5 between terms.

```javascript
db.movies.aggregate([
  {
    $search: {
      "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
      "phrase": {
        "path": "plot",
        "query": "off"
      }
    }
  }
])

```

regex

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the regular expression pattern.

```javascript
db.movies.aggregate([
  {
    $search: {
      "regex": "partial-match-tutorial",
      "phrase": {
        "path": "plot",
        "query": "off"
      }
    }
  }
])

```

wildcard

The following query searches for documents where the `plot` field
contains words that match the wildcard pattern.

```javascript
db.movies.aggregate([
  {
    $search: {
      "index": "partial-match-tutorial",
      "wildcard": {
        "path": "plot",
        "query": "how*"
      }
    }
  }
])

```

This tutorial describes how to create an index on the
`sample_mflix.movies` collection and run partial string queries
against the `plot` field using the C driver in a local deployment.

To return matches for partial string queries, you can use one of the following operators:

- The [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for a word or phrase that contains the sequence of
  characters that you specify with your query.

- The [Phrase](phrase-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for documents that contain the terms in your query
  string at the distance you specify between the terms.

- The [regex](regex-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields for strings using regular expression.

- The [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator, which allows you to search the
  specified fields using special characters in your query to match any
  character.

You can also use the [text](text-ref) operator with a custom analyzer
for more fine-grained control over partial matching. To learn more, see
[Custom analyzers](custom-analyzers).

This tutorial takes you through the following steps:

- Set up a MongoDB Search index on the `plot` field in the
  `sample_mflix.movies` collection.

- Run MongoDB Search query for a partial string against the `plot` field in
  the `sample_mflix.movies` collection using
  [autocomplete](autocomplete-ref), [Phrase](phrase-ref), [regex](regex-ref), and
  [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operators.

Before you begin, ensure that your cluster meets the following requirements.

To complete the tutorials, you must have:

- An Atlas cluster with MongoDB version 6.0 or higher or a MongoDB
  self-managed cluster with MongoDB version 8.2 or higher.

- The [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data) loaded into your
  cluster.

sections.heading: Create the MongoDB Search Index
Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
Create the MongoDB Search Index
Run a Case-Sensitive Partial Match Query
Create the MongoDB Search Index
sections.body:
You can use the MongoDB Search Visual Editor or the MongoDB Search JSON
Editor in the Atlas user interface to create the index.

Visual Editor

- Click Refine Your Index.

- In the Index Configurations section, toggle to
  disable Dynamic Mapping.

- In the Field Mappings section, click
  Add Field to display the Add Field
  Mapping window.

- Configure the field mapping based on the operator you intend to use:

  autocomplete

  - Click Quick Start for Text Fields.

  - Select the Search-as-you-type
    template from the dropdown and review the
    default settings.

    To learn more about these settings, see
    [tokenization](bson-data-types-autocomplete).

  phrase

  - Click Quick Start for Text Fields.

  - Select the Full Text Search template
    from the dropdown and review the default
    settings.

    To learn more about these settings, see
    [string](bson-data-types-string).

  regex

  - Select Customized Configuration from
    the dropdown.

  - Select String from the
    Data Type dropdown to run the query
    using the [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator.

  - Modify the default settings for the data type as shown
    below:

    | UI Field Name   | Configuration  |
    | --------------- | -------------- |
    | Index Analyzer  | lucene.keyword |
    | Search Analyzer | lucene.keyword |
    | Index Options   | offsets        |
    | Store           | true           |
    | Ignore Above    | None           |
    | Norms           | include        |

    To learn more about these settings, see
    [string](bson-data-types-string).

  wildcard

  - Select Customized Configuration from
    the dropdown.

  - Select String from the
    Data Type dropdown to run the query
    using the [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator.

  - Modify the default settings for the data type as shown
    below:

    | UI Field Name   | Configuration  |
    | --------------- | -------------- |
    | Index Analyzer  | lucene.keyword |
    | Search Analyzer | lucene.keyword |
    | Index Options   | offsets        |
    | Store           | true           |
    | Ignore Above    | None           |
    | Norms           | include        |

    To learn more about these settings, see
    [string](bson-data-types-string).

- Select `plot` from the Field Name dropdown.

- Click Add to add the field to the list in
  Field Mappings section.

- Click Save Changes.

JSON Editor

- Replace the default index definition with the following example
  index definition for the operator you intend to use for running
  the query.

  autocomplete

  ```json
  {
    "mappings": {
      "dynamic": false,
      "fields": {
        "plot": [
          {
            "type": "autocomplete",
            "tokenization": "edgeGram",
            "minGrams": 2,
            "maxGrams": 15,
            "foldDiacritics": true
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  phrase

  ```json
  {
    "mappings": {
      "fields": {
        "plot": {
          "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
          "type": "string"
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  regex

  ```json
  {
    "mappings": {
      "fields": {
        "plot": {
          "analyzer": "lucene.keyword",
          "type": "string"
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  wildcard

  ```json
  {
    "mappings": {
      "fields": {
        "plot": {
          "analyzer": "lucene.keyword",
          "type": "string"
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

- Click Next.

A modal window appears to let you know your index is building. Click
the Close button.

The index should take about one minute to build. While it is
building, the Status column reads `Build in
Progress`. When it is finished building, the
Status column reads `Active`.

codeBlocks.content:
Title: Panic Room
Plot: A divorced woman and her diabetic daughter take refuge in their newly-purchased house's safe room, when three men break-in, searching for a missing fortune.

Title: Her
Plot: A lonely writer develops an unlikely relationship with his newly purchased operating system that's designed to meet his every need.

Title: Miracles - Mr. Canton and Lady Rose
Plot: A country boy becomes the head of a gang through the purchase of some lucky roses from an old lady. He and a singer at the gang's nightclub try to do a good deed for the old lady when her daughter comes to visit.

Title: Repo Men
Plot: Set in the near future when artificial organs can be bought on credit, it revolves around a man who struggles to make the payments on a heart he has purchased. He must therefore go on the run before said ticker is repossessed.

Title: Punch-Drunk Love
Plot: A psychologically troubled novelty supplier is nudged towards a romance with an English woman, all the while being extorted by a phone-sex line run by a crooked mattress salesman, and purchasing stunning amounts of pudding.

Title: Music Within
Plot: The true story of Richard Pimentel, a brilliant public speaker with a troubled past, who returns from Vietnam severely hearing -impaired and finds a new purpose in his landmark efforts on the behalf of Americans with disabilities.

Title: Megamind
Plot: The supervillain Megamind finally defeats his nemesis, the superhero Metro Man. But without a hero, he loses all purpose and must find new meaning to his life.

Title: Pat Garrett & Billy the Kid
Plot: An aging Pat Garrett is hired as a lawman on behalf of a group of wealthy New Mexico cattle barons--his sole purpose being to bring down his old friend Billy the Kid.

Title: Star Wars: Episode V - The Empire Strikes Back
Plot: After the rebels have been brutally overpowered by the Empire on their newly established base, Luke Skywalker takes advanced Jedi training with Master Yoda, while his friends are pursued by Darth Vader as part of his plan to capture Luke.

Title: Major League
Plot: The new owner of the Cleveland Indians puts together a purposely horrible team so they'll lose and she can move the team. But when the plot is uncovered, they start winning just to spite her.

Title: Music Within
Plot: The true story of Richard Pimentel, a brilliant public speaker with a troubled past, who returns from Vietnam severely hearing -impaired and finds a new purpose in his landmark efforts on the behalf of Americans with disabilities.

Title: Her
Plot: A lonely writer develops an unlikely relationship with his newly purchased operating system that's designed to meet his every need.

Title: Kick
Plot: An adrenaline junkie walks away from a whirlwind romance and embraces a new life as a thief, though he soon finds himself pursued by veteran police officer and engaged in a turf war with a local gangster.

Title: Star Wars: Episode V - The Empire Strikes Back
Plot: After the rebels have been brutally overpowered by the Empire on their newly established base, Luke Skywalker takes advanced Jedi training with Master Yoda, while his friends are pursued by Darth Vader as part of his plan to capture Luke.

Title: Major League
Plot: The new owner of the Cleveland Indians puts together a purposely horrible team so they'll lose and she can move the team. But when the plot is uncovered, they start winning just to spite her.

Title: Music Within
Plot: The true story of Richard Pimentel, a brilliant public speaker with a troubled past, who returns from Vietnam severely hearing -impaired and finds a new purpose in his landmark efforts on the behalf of Americans with disabilities.

Title: Her
Plot: A lonely writer develops an unlikely relationship with his newly purchased operating system that's designed to meet his every need.

Title: Kick
Plot: An adrenaline junkie walks away from a whirlwind romance and embraces a new life as a thief, though he soon finds himself pursued by veteran police officer and engaged in a turf war with a local gangster.

codeBlocks.content:
Title: Panic Room
Plot: A divorced woman and her diabetic daughter take refuge in their newly-purchased house's safe room, when three men break-in, searching for a missing fortune.

Title: Her
Plot: A lonely writer develops an unlikely relationship with his newly purchased operating system that's designed to meet his every need.

Title: Miracles - Mr. Canton and Lady Rose
Plot: A country boy becomes the head of a gang through the purchase of some lucky roses from an old lady. He and a singer at the gang's nightclub try to do a good deed for the old lady when her daughter comes to visit.

Title: Repo Men
Plot: Set in the near future when artificial organs can be bought on credit, it revolves around a man who struggles to make the payments on a heart he has purchased. He must therefore go on the run before said ticker is repossessed.

Title: Punch-Drunk Love
Plot: A psychologically troubled novelty supplier is nudged towards a romance with an English woman, all the while being extorted by a phone-sex line run by a crooked mattress salesman, and purchasing stunning amounts of pudding.

Title: Music Within
Plot: The true story of Richard Pimentel, a brilliant public speaker with a troubled past, who returns from Vietnam severely hearing -impaired and finds a new purpose in his landmark efforts on the behalf of Americans with disabilities.

Title: Megamind
Plot: The supervillain Megamind finally defeats his nemesis, the superhero Metro Man. But without a hero, he loses all purpose and must find new meaning to his life.

Title: Pat Garrett & Billy the Kid
Plot: An aging Pat Garrett is hired as a lawman on behalf of a group of wealthy New Mexico cattle barons--his sole purpose being to bring down his old friend Billy the Kid.
